examples.title: Basic tree
examples.description: A small hierarchy with one branch expanded. Uses the Tree namespace API: Tree.Item composes inside the parent Tree.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Tree } from '@lostgradient/cinder/tree';

  let expandedIds = $state<string[]>(['fruit']);
</script>

<Tree aria-label="Pantry" bind:expandedIds>
  <Tree.Item id="fruit" label="Fruit" branch>
    <Tree.Item id="apple" label="Apple" />
    <Tree.Item id="banana" label="Banana" />
    <Tree.Item id="citrus" label="Citrus" branch>
      <Tree.Item id="lemon" label="Lemon" />
      <Tree.Item id="orange" label="Orange" />
    </Tree.Item>
  </Tree.Item>
  <Tree.Item id="grain" label="Grain" branch>
    <Tree.Item id="rice" label="Rice" />
    <Tree.Item id="wheat" label="Wheat" />
  </Tree.Item>
</Tree>

examples.title: Cascading toggle
examples.description: Branch activation can opt into selecting or clearing the branch and its explicit selection scope.
examples.code: Toggle the docs branch checkbox to select or clear its full scope.

examples.title: File explorer multi-select
examples.description: A file-tree pattern with checkbox indicators, a root-level select-all control, and bindable selected ids.
examples.code:
{#snippet selectionControls()} {/snippet}

Selected: {selectedIds.length > 0 ? selectedIds.join(', ') : 'none'}

examples.title: Indeterminate parents
examples.description: Parent checkbox indicators move to mixed state when only part of their selection scope is selected.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Tree } from '@lostgradient/cinder/tree';

  let selectedIds = $state<string[]>(['february']);
  let expandedIds = $state<string[]>(['archive']);
</script>

<Tree
  aria-label="Archived reports"
  selectionMode="multiple"
  checkboxSelection
  selectionBehavior="cascade"
  bind:selectedIds
  bind:expandedIds
>
  <Tree.Item
    id="archive"
    label="archive"
    branch
    selectionScopeIds={['archive', 'january', 'february']}
  >
    <Tree.Item id="january" label="january.pdf" />
    <Tree.Item id="february" label="february.pdf" />
  </Tree.Item>
  <Tree.Item id="summary" label="summary.pdf" />
</Tree>

examples.title: Long labels and narrow widths
examples.description: Long item labels truncate with an ellipsis rather than forcing the tree wider than its container. The full text stays available to assistive tech via the visually-hidden label. Resize narrow to see truncation; rows keep a comfortable touch target.

examples.title: Drag reorder
examples.description: Draggable tree items emit a consumer-owned reorder target. The example records the last drop so pointer behavior can be verified.
examples.code:
{#each items as item (item.id)} {#if item.branch} {:else} {/if} {/each}

{lastDrop}

examples.title: Virtualized tree
examples.description: A data-driven tree renders a small window from a 10,000-item collection while preserving full aria-posinset and aria-setsize metadata.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Tree, type TreeDataItem } from '@lostgradient/cinder/tree';

  const items: TreeDataItem[] = Array.from({ length: 10_000 }, (_, index) => ({
    id: `virtual-file-${index}`,
    label: `Virtual file ${index}`,
  }));
</script>

<Tree
  aria-label="Virtualized files"
  {items}
  virtualized
  virtualizationEstimatedRowHeight={32}
  virtualizationHeight={320}
  style="border: 1px solid var(--cinder-border); border-radius: var(--cinder-radius-md);"
/>
